content.text:
Analyze the codebase for issues and problems.

Scope: Full codebase examination. Look at:
- Structure and organization
- Code quality patterns
- Type safety issues (if TypeScript)
- Error handling gaps
- Security concerns
- Performance issues
- Dependency problems
- Configuration issues
- Testing gaps
- Any broken or incomplete implementations
- Dead code or TODOs
- Obvious bugs or logic errors

Acceptance criteria:
- Comprehensive issue list organized by category (type safety, error handling, security, performance, etc.)
- For each issue: location (file/module), severity (high/medium/low), description, and suggested fix
- Prioritized by impact/severity
- Quick wins highlighted separately

Constraints:
- Focus on substantive issues, not style/linting
- Avoid generic suggestions — be specific about location and fix
- Include only issues you can verify by reading code

Constraints:
